title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Program Brief: Secret Privacy Scan
summary: Raw HTML executes author input, javascript links survive, a template loader escapes its root, copied SVG contains active content, secrets enter output, and CSP is treated as the only XSS defense. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the secret privacy scan increment in explicit static-publishing trust and security policy. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.
Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 9875 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-secret-privacy-scan — Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_security_trust_4(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Secret Privacy Scan evidence · Theory Secret Privacy Scan
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-secret-privacy-scan-19cvwp" that uses case 7345 to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Secret Privacy Scan evidence · Theory Secret Privacy Scan.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Secret Privacy Scan into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Secret Privacy Scan evidence · Theory Secret Privacy Scan, keep Secret Privacy Scan connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-secret-privacy-scan-19cvwp
# Competency: ssg-secret-privacy-scan.
# Case 7345: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_secret_privacy_scan_19cvwp(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_19cvwp = "19cvwp-pwvc91-7345"
    assert evidence_variant_19cvwp
    evidence_axes_19cvwp = (
        "reader19cvwp",
        "source7345",
        "parsepwvc91",
        "route19cvwp7345",
        "artifact734519cvwp",
        "recoverypwvc917345",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_19cvwp)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Secret Privacy Scan: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9252 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.
